name: Workflow C - Queue Processor multi user

parameters.jsCode:
function stripHtml(html) { return String(html || '') .replace(//gi, '\n') .replace(/<\/p>\s*

/gi, '\n\n') .replace(/<\/li>\s*

  • /gi, '\n- ') .replace(/
  • /gi, '- ') .replace(/<\/(p|div|ul|ol|li|h\d)>/gi, '\n') .replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '') .replace(/ /g, ' ') .replace(/&/g, '&') .replace(/</g, '<') .replace(/>/g, '>') .replace(/"/g, '"') .replace(/'/g, "'") .replace(/\n{3,}/g, '\n\n') .trim(); } function pad2(n) { const num = Number(n); if (!Number.isFinite(num)) return ''; return String(num).padStart(2, '0'); } function toDatePosted(day, month, year) { const y = String(year ?? '').trim(); const m = pad2(month); const d = pad2(day); if (!y || !m || !d) return ''; return `${d}-${m}-${y}`; } const row = $json || {}; const title = row['Job Title'] || row['Title'] || ''; const company = row['Company'] || ''; const location = row['Location'] || ''; const seniority = row['Seniority'] || '' || ''; const datePosted = row['Date Posted'] || ''; const descriptionText = stripHtml(row['Description'] || ''); const customPrompt = row.customPrompt || ''; const outputChatId = row.outputChatId || ''; const userName = row.userName || ''; const jobInput = `\n\n# Input\n\nAdvert text:\n${descriptionText}\n\nCompany: ${company}\nJob title: ${title}\nLocation: ${location}\nDate posted: ${datePosted}`; let fullPrompt; if (customPrompt.trim()) { fullPrompt = customPrompt + jobInput; } else { fullPrompt = `Job title: ${title} Location: ${location} Date posted: ${datePosted} Instruction: # Role You are a senior research analyst at an elite agency recruiter. You will be given a public job description, which you should treat it as the primary signal # Research Scope Use publicly available information to understand the company and its wholesale credit context across buy-side and sell-side perspectives, including: -Company structure, ownership, governance, filings, and group relationships -The hiring company’s website and investor materials -Recent news, leadership changes, and events affecting leverage, liquidity, covenants, refinancing, capital allocation, or risk governance -Regulatory, legal, and ESG issues relevant to wholesale credit risk -Public market signals such as equity performance, bond or CDS commentary, and changes in consensus expectations Prioritise issuer disclosures, investor relations material, filings, prospectuses, rating agency releases, and reputable financial press. Avoid buzzwords without explanation and speculation not grounded in public signals. Your task is not to sell or pitch. Your task is to explain what is really happening, in plain English, and build the understanding a recruiter needs to become useful later if hiring stalls. # Strategic Framing (critical) Assume: 1. The company does not want recruiters initially 2. Multiple recruiters are already involved 3. Inbound volume is high 4. Speed and access are not differentiators 5. The recruiter’s edge is interpretation, timing, and framing 6. The goal is to be useful later, not noisy now # Objective Produce a Hiring Intelligence Dossier that allows the recruiter to: 1. Understand the company and role beyond the job description 2. Explain why this role exists now 3. Identify where the hiring process is likely to fail 4. Anticipate what the company will only realise after trying to hire alone 5. Build the basis for a future insight-led outreach message # Output Produce a Hiring Intelligence Dossier. Explain all jargon the first time it appears. Do not restate the job description. ## 1. Company and Market Context In plain English, explain: -What the company does and how it generates cash that services debt -Its market position versus peers and its credit profile where observable -How it is financed across public and private markets and who its lenders or investors are -What matters right now for credit risk, such as refinancing, M&A, covenant pressure, or liquidity changes -Any recent events that make this hire necessary now -External pressures affecting wholesale credit outcomes, including regulation, funding costs, market windows, and investor risk appetite -Market mechanics that affect execution, such as underwriting, syndication, and distribution risk ## 2. The Core Business Problem Explain the real problem beneath the role and what the business risks losing if the hire is delayed, typically revenue or balance sheet control. ## 3. Stated Need vs Actual Need Provide a two-column table: | What the JD Asks For | What the Business Likely Needs | Highlight gaps and misalignment in plain language. ## 4. Specification and Execution Risk Identify and explain: -Whether there is over-specification and unrealistic scope -If three are conflicting expectations such as discipline versus growth or independence versus distribution -Is there a mismatch between responsibility, authority, and accountability -Are theree any signals that materially reduce the talent pool -What is the pay versus market and are there any mismatches if disclosed -Whether the firm’s reputation or franchise strength is insufficient to attract the profile required ## 5. Ideal Candidate Profiles Describe two strong-fit candidate types. For each, explain: -Where they typically come from -Why they fit this situation -What outcomes they could realistically deliver ## 6. Recruiter Search Boolean Provide copy-paste-ready Boolean strings only, with no explanation, for: -Core titles -Adjacent or non-obvious titles -Keywords -Target companies as a single OR string ## 7. Lead Score (1–5) - Include a 2–3 sentence justification Assign one overall score from 1–5 representing: How worthwhile this company and role would be for a high-quality agency recruiter to invest meaningful time in, assuming the company initially resists recruiters and only engages if hiring becomes difficult. This score is a filter, not a compliment. ## 8. Who’s Hiring Manager? 1. Decide what role the hiring manager has (this could be up to 10, but fewer if you are more confident). a. As this is a credit risk role and the hiring manager will be the Head of Credit, so only look for any senior people in the company who's title contains the word "credit", for example, Head of Credit, Deputy Head of Credit, Chief Credit Officer or Credit Risk Director. If you can't find a senior "credit" person, the next best thing is the "Chief Risk Officer" i. This is non negotiable. Only return people who are leaders with the word "credit" or, 'cheif risk officer' 2. Using the above criteria, try to figure out the exact hiring manager and narrow it down to specific people, share their names with a linkedin URL. ## 9. Outreach Messages Write a five-step SourceWhale email sequence consisting of an initial message and four follow-ups. For the initial email use professional, direct, plain British English. Position the sender as a specialist recruiter with a strong network and long track record. Keep the technical jargon to a minimum and the focus squarely on the recruitment pain points, such as candidate shortages, fierce competition for top talent, and any other relevant factors that might mean the employer struggles to attract talent. Aim to differentiate the sender from the competition by emphasising their long standing track record and extensive network in credit risk recruitment. The follow up emails should all be more generic, recruitment-focused and lay off the technicals. Do not include any sign-off or signature in any outreach message. #Constraints - Use external research only for context and validation - Do not restate or summarise the JD - Explain all jargon and assumptions - Be decisive, commercial, and clear - Don’t exactly parrot the job title or anything else in the JD, humans don’t write this way. Condense if possible - No em-dashes, or emboldenings. #Rules You will be given job metadata fields: title, company, datePosted, location. Return JSON that includes these four fields at the top level AND an "output" object. Copy title, company, datePosted, location exactly as provided. Do not edit them. # Input Advert text: ${descriptionText} Company: ${company} Job title: ${title} Location: ${location} Date posted: ${datePosted} `; } return { title, company, location, seniority, datePosted, descriptionText, customPrompt, outputChatId, userName, fullPrompt };

options.systemMessage: To follow the prompt to the letter

parameters.inputSchema:
{
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "title": { "type": "string" },
    "company": { "type": "string" },
    "datePosted": { "type": "string" },
    "location": { "type": "string" },
    "output": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "Candidate Search Boolean": { "type": "string" },
        "Hiring Manager Search Boolean": { "type": "string" },
        "Hiring Manager Name": { "type": "string" },
        "Outreach 1": { "type": "string" },
        "Outreach 2": { "type": "string" },
        "Outreach 3": { "type": "string" },
        "Outreach 4": { "type": "string" },
        "Outreach 5": { "type": "string" }
      },
      "required": [
        "Candidate Search Boolean",
        "Hiring Manager Search Boolean",
        "Hiring Manager Name",
        "Outreach 1",
        "Outreach 2",
        "Outreach 3",
        "Outreach 4",
        "Outreach 5"
      ],
      "additionalProperties": false
    }
  },
  "required": ["title", "company", "datePosted", "location", "output"],
  "additionalProperties": false
}
